feature.name: Update Device Memory API limits
feature.creator: barrypollard@google.com
feature.summary:
Update to a new set of possible values for the Device Memory API:
- Android: 1, 2, 4, 8
- Others: 2, 4, 8, 16, 32
Replacing the old values of 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8 which have grown outdated.

This will reduce the fingerprinting risks at the lower end since device capabilities have improved since these were set.

It will also allow better usage and segmenting of high-end devices as requested by developers (https://github.com/w3c/device-memory/issues/50).

adoption.finding: The legacy HTML/JS counter 'NavigatorDeviceMemory' (ChromeStatus timeline, bucket_id 2121) measured 41.9032% of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads on 2026-07-20. Tracked since 2017-08-29 with 3035 data points. Mapping confidence: exact.
adoption.limitations: Counter measures this feature directly but may include pre-existing usage.

deprecation.finding: This is a capability addition or behavior change, not a deprecation.
